politicsNews.author: Margaret Holloway
politicsNews.title: UK Local Elections: US Officials Watch Closely as Starmer Faces Populist Surge
politicsNews.description: As polls open across Britain, Washington is tracking the potential shift in Westminster's power balance and its ripple effect on the 'Special Relationship.'

politicsNews.author: Margaret Holloway
politicsNews.title: Senate Cloture Vote: Kevin Warsh Set for 14-Year Federal Reserve Term
politicsNews.description: The U.S. Senate proceeds with a high-stakes vote on Kevin Warsh's nomination to the Board of Governors, signaling a major long-term shift in American monetary policy.

politicsNews.author: Margaret Holloway
politicsNews.title: Bipartisan Push: Congress Moves to Raise Capitol Police Retirement Age
politicsNews.description: Facing a massive surge in lawmaker threats, the Senate votes to extend the retirement age for Capitol Police to tackle critical staffing shortages.

secondaryNews.author: Margaret Holloway
secondaryNews.title: Speaker Mike Johnson Backs Massive 'Rededicate 250' Prayer Rally in DC
secondaryNews.description: Thousands filled the National Mall for a daylong Christian jubilee backed by the White House and headlined by Speaker Johnson, VP Vance, and top Cabinet officials.

secondaryNews.author: Margaret Holloway
secondaryNews.title: Green Party's Hannah Spencer Officially Enters House of Commons
secondaryNews.description: Newly elected Green MP Hannah Spencer officially takes her seat in the House of Commons this week following a closely watched by-election win in Gorton and Denton.

secondaryNews.author: Margaret Holloway
secondaryNews.title: House Ethics Panel Launches Investigation Into Rep. Nancy Mace
secondaryNews.description: Capitol Hill faces a new inquiry as the House Ethics Committee officially opens an investigation into Representative Nancy Mace.

featured.author: Carlos Medina Reyes
featured.title: MLB Star Under Fire: Dodgers Pitcher Edwin Díaz Linked to Puerto Rico Cockfighting Ring
featured.quote: A USA Today investigation ties Dodgers closer Edwin Díaz to illegal cockfighting events in Puerto Rico, with arena photos and social media ads surfacing despite a federal ban carrying up to five years in prison.

grid.author: Carlos Medina Reyes
grid.title: Polaris Gets the Go-Ahead for a Big Battery Storage Project in Puerto Rico
grid.description: PREPA has agreed to a 71.4MW battery energy storage system deal with Polaris Renewable Energy.

grid.author: Carlos Medina Reyes
grid.title: Ricky Castillo Claims First PGA Tour Victory at Puerto Rico Open
grid.description: Pro golfer Ricky Castillo secured his first career PGA Tour win in Rio Grande this weekend.

healthcareNews.title: Lord Hogan-Howe to Lead Landmark Review of Police Accountability and Structures

main.author: Simone Varlette
main.title: Privacy Backtrack: Instagram Scraps Encrypted DM Feature Under Fire
main.description: Meta has stripped end-to-end encryption from Instagram direct messages, quietly removing a feature introduced in 2023 and handing itself the technical ability to read every private conversation on the platform.

sidebar.author: Simone Varlette
sidebar.title: Trump Administration Releases 2026 National AI Policy Blueprint

sidebar.author: Simone Varlette
sidebar.title: Apple Issues Emergency iOS Patch to Stop DarkSword Spyware

newsCards.title: Takeda Hit With $885 Million Verdict Over Generic Drug Delay Scheme
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: A federal jury in Boston found Takeda liable for blocking generic competition to its constipation drug Amitiza — the first pay-for-delay antitrust verdict against a pharma company in U.S. class-action history.

newsCards.title: Connecticut Clinic Accused in $659K Medicaid Fraud Scheme
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: Four people tied to a Shelton pain clinic face charges over an alleged scheme in which APRM Solutions submitted 4,009 false Medicaid claims for allergy treatments that were never performed or carried out without proper licensing.

newsCards.title: CEO and Owners Sentenced in Huge $92 Million Black-Market HIV Drug Ring
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: The CEO and owners of a pharmaceutical wholesaler were sentenced to decades in prison for running a $92 million black-market HIV drug ring.

newsCards.title: Aetna to Pay $117.7 Million to Settle Medicare Advantage Fraud Allegations
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: Aetna agreed to pay $117.7 million to settle False Claims Act violations after allegedly inflating patient risk scores to boost federal payments.

newsCards.title: Texas Medical Equipment Owner Sentenced for $59 Million Medicare Scam
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: A Texas DME company owner was sentenced to prison and ordered to pay restitution for running a $59 million Medicare kickback scheme.

newsCards.title: Owner of Oregon Clinical Laboratory Charged in $46 Million Medicare Fraud
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: A Pakistani national was arrested at LAX while fleeing the country after allegedly billing Medicare $46 million for lab tests never performed.

newsCards.title: SCOTUS Allows $7 Billion RICO Fraud Case Against Takeda and Eli Lilly to Proceed
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: The Supreme Court cleared the way for a $7 billion RICO suit alleging Takeda and Eli Lilly hid cancer risks from patients and regulators.

newsCards.title: Florida Doctor Pleads Guilty in Multi-Million Dollar Telehealth Fraud Scheme
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: Dr. Grinshteyn pleaded guilty to approving thousands of unnecessary genetic tests and equipment orders through a fraudulent telemedicine network.

newsCards.title: Federal Court Revives Lawsuit Against AbbVie and AstraZeneca Over Drug Overcharging
newsCards.author: Ruth Anselmi
newsCards.excerpt: The Ninth Circuit revived a qui tam lawsuit claiming AbbVie and AstraZeneca systematically overcharged public health programs for life-saving drugs.

discoveryMain.title: Global Rainforests Near Breaking Point as Resource Exploitation Surges
discoveryMain.author: Thomas Aldgate
discoveryMain.excerpt: A new Rainforest Foundation Norway report finds that rising demand for critical minerals, biofuels, and industrial pulp is layering fresh extraction pressure on already-stressed forests across the Amazon, Congo Basin, and Southeast Asia.

discoveryMiddle.title: Canadian Firm Proposes New Uranium Drilling in New Mexico's Chama Valley
discoveryMiddle.author: Thomas Aldgate

discoveryMiddle.title: New Law Slashes Federal Oil Royalties to Record Lows for Drillers
discoveryMiddle.author: Thomas Aldgate

discoveryRight.author: Thomas Aldgate

discoveryRight.title: New Data Reveals U.K. Rivers Hit by 300,000 Sewage Spills Despite Dry Year
discoveryRight.author: Thomas Aldgate

discoveryRight.title: Iconic Cornwall Tin Mine Hits Critical Milestone for 2026 Production Restart
discoveryRight.author: Thomas Aldgate

discoveryRight.title: U.K. Farmers Lobby Against New Restrictions on Controversial Antibiotic Use
discoveryRight.author: Thomas Aldgate

technologyNews.title: IRGC Intelligence Chief Killed in Tehran Amid U.S.–Iran Ceasefire Talks
technologyNews.excerpt: Washington and Tehran receive a 45-day ceasefire draft as Iran's top intelligence commander falls in a targeted strike inside the capital.
technologyNews.author: Naomi Vosburgh

technologyNews.title: Chinese Tech Firms Exposing U.S Military Secrets in the Iran Conflict
technologyNews.excerpt: A 2026 intelligence alert warns Chinese tech firms are mining open-source data to unmask covert U.S operations and compromise field teams.
technologyNews.author: Naomi Vosburgh
